description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage, painted predominantly white with a blue and gold livery and the Singapore Airlines logo on the tail fin. The aircraft features two large turbofan engines mounted under its low, swept-back wings, which are equipped with upward-angled winglets at the tips. The nose is rounded and houses a forward-positioned, multi-windowed cockpit, while the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and stowed within the fuselage and wings. The windows along the fuselage indicate multiple rows of passenger seating, typical of a wide-body jet. The overall design is sleek and aerodynamic, optimized for long-distance travel.
description: A large, white commercial passenger airplane with blue and gold accents, featuring underwing turbofan engines, swept low wings with winglets, and a Singapore Airlines livery.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the Singapore Airlines branding, and the tail logo.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the wingspan, winglets, and the overall planform of the airplane.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the fuselage, engines, and tail design.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetry of the wings and engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with long, swept-back wings featuring upward-angled winglets and moderate dihedral.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle (not visible, inferred from typical design)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows
